coredata.dc:title: State of Charge (SOC) Estimation of Lithium-ion Battery Based on Adaptive Square Root Unscented Kalman Filter
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Kai, Wang
Xiao, Feng
Jinbo, Pang
Jun, Ren
Chongxiong, Duan
Liwei, Li
coredata.dc:description: The improved battery management system (BMS) can give full play to the best performance of power battery, and the state of charge (SOC) estimation of power lithium-ion battery is the core and key technology of BMS. The Kalman filter method with the first-order Thevenin model cannot obtain better estimation results because of the limited model precision. Aiming at solving the above problems, this paper presents a second-order Thevenin equivalent circuit model. The idea of the Sage-Husa adaptive algorithm and square root filter is introduced based on the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) algorithm. The adaptive square root Unscented Kalman Filter (ASRUKF) algorithm is formed to improve the precision of SOC estimation. Experiments on SOC estimation of the battery are carried out under three different working conditions. The experimental results show that the ASRUKF algorithm under the second-order Thevenin equivalent circuit model can converge quickly and achieve high precision in SOC estimation.
coredata.dcterms:subject: lithium-ion battery
SOC estimation
equivalent circuit model
adaptive square root Unscented Kalman Filter

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: State of Charge (SOC) Estimation of Lithium-ion Battery Based on Adaptive Square Root Unscented Kalman Filter Wang Kai, 1 wkwj888@163.com Feng Xiao, 1 Pang Jinbo, 2 Ren Jun, 1 Duan Chongxiong, 3 Li Liwei, 1 ytllw@163.com 1 School of Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China School of Electrical Engineering Qingdao University Qingdao 266071 China School of Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China 2 Collaborative Innovation Center of Technology and Equipment for Biological Diagnosis and Therapy in Universities of Shandong, Institute for Advanced Interdisciplinary Research (iAIR), University of Jinan, Shandong, Jinan 250022, China Collaborative Innovation Center of Technology and Equipment for Biological Diagnosis and Therapy in Universities of Shandong, Institute for Advanced Interdisciplinary Research (iAIR) University of Jinan Jinan Shandong 250022 China Collaborative Innovation Center of Technology and Equipment for Biological Diagnosis and Therapy in Universities of Shandong, Institute for Advanced Interdisciplinary Research (iAIR), University of Jinan, Shandong, Jinan 250022, China 3 School of Materials Science and Energy Engineering, Foshan University, Foshan, 528231, China School of Materials Science and Energy Engineering Foshan University Foshan 528231 China School of Materials Science and Energy Engineering, Foshan University, Foshan, 528231, China The improved battery management system (BMS) can give full play to the best performance of power battery, and the state of charge (SOC) estimation of power lithium-ion battery is the core and key technology of BMS. The Kalman filter method with the first-order Thevenin model cannot obtain better estimation results because of the limited model precision. Aiming at solving the above problems, this paper presents a second-order Thevenin equivalent circuit model. The idea of the Sage-Husa adaptive algorithm and square root filter is introduced based on the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) algorithm. The adaptive square root Unscented Kalman Filter (ASRUKF) algorithm is formed to improve the precision of SOC estimation. Experiments on SOC estimation of the battery are carried out under three different working conditions. The experimental results show that the ASRUKF algorithm under the second-order Thevenin equivalent circuit model can converge quickly and achieve high precision in SOC estimation. Keywords: lithium-ion battery SOC estimation equivalent circuit model adaptive square root Unscented Kalman Filter References 1. M.A. Hannan, M.S.H. Lipu, A. Hussain, Renew. Sust. Energ. Rev. 78: (2017) 834-854 M. A. Hannan, M. S. H. Lipu and A. Hussain, Renew. Sust. Energ. Rev., 78 (2017) 834-854. 2. Y. Wang, C. Zhang, Z. Chen, Appl. Energy 185: (2017) 2026-2032 Y. Wang, C. Zhang and Z. Chen, Appl. Energy, 185 (2017) 2026-2032. 3. K. Wang, L. Li, H. Zhang, Int. J. Electrochem. Sci. 8: (2013) 5036-5041 K. Wang, L. Li and H. Zhang, Int. J. Electrochem. Sci., 8 (2013) 5036-5041. 4. Z. Cabrane, M. Ouassaid, M. Maaroufi, IET Renew. Power Gener. 11: (2017) 1157-1165 Z. Cabrane, M. Ouassaid and M. Maaroufi, IET Renew. Power Gener., 11 (2017) 1157-1165. 5. Y.T. Zhou, Y.N. Wang, K. Wang, L. Kang, F. Peng, L.C. Wang, J.B. Pang, Appl. Energy 260: (2020) 114169 Y. T. Zhou, Y. N. Wang, K. Wang, L. Kang, F. Peng, L. C. D. Rao, G. Lu, B. Li,

simple-head.ce:title: State of Charge (SOC) Estimation of Lithium-ion Battery Based on Adaptive Square Root Unscented Kalman Filter

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Electrical Engineering, Qingdao University, Qingdao, 266071, China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Collaborative Innovation Center of Technology and Equipment for Biological Diagnosis and Therapy in Universities of Shandong, Institute for Advanced Interdisciplinary Research (iAIR), University of Jinan, Shandong, Jinan 250022, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Collaborative Innovation Center of Technology and Equipment for Biological Diagnosis and Therapy in Universities of Shandong, Institute for Advanced Interdisciplinary Research (iAIR)

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Collaborative Innovation Center of Technology and Equipment for Biological Diagnosis and Therapy in Universities of Shandong, Institute for Advanced Interdisciplinary Research (iAIR), University of Jinan, Shandong, Jinan 250022, China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Materials Science and Energy Engineering, Foshan University, Foshan, 528231, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Materials Science and Energy Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Materials Science and Energy Engineering, Foshan University, Foshan, 528231, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The improved battery management system (BMS) can give full play to the best performance of power battery, and the state of charge (SOC) estimation of power lithium-ion battery is the core and key technology of BMS. The Kalman filter method with the first-order Thevenin model cannot obtain better estimation results because of the limited model precision. Aiming at solving the above problems, this paper presents a second-order Thevenin equivalent circuit model. The idea of the Sage-Husa adaptive algorithm and square root filter is introduced based on the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) algorithm. The adaptive square root Unscented Kalman Filter (ASRUKF) algorithm is formed to improve the precision of SOC estimation. Experiments on SOC estimation of the battery are carried out under three different working conditions. The experimental results show that the ASRUKF algorithm under the second-order Thevenin equivalent circuit model can converge quickly and achieve high precision in SOC estimation.

ce:keyword.ce:text: adaptive square root Unscented Kalman Filter
